channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Diplomatic Stalemate as Russia Insists on Territorial Demands Despite Setbacks
item.description: Four years into the ongoing war in Ukraine, diplomatic efforts to reach a resolution remain slow and ineffective. Russian President Vladimir Putin continues to believe he can achieve victory, maintain...

item.title: Research on Experienced Buddhist Monks Shows Meditation Increases Brain Signal Complexity and Shifts Neural Dynamics Toward Criticality
item.description: A recent international study published in Neuroscience of Consciousness examined brain activity in 12 highly experienced Buddhist monks from the Thai Forest Tradition using magnetoencephalography (MEG...

item.title: Top 10 TV Shows to Watch This February
item.description: February brings an exciting mix of TV shows to stream across various platforms, with a focus on diverse genres. From post-apocalyptic adventures like *Fallout*, to the humorous and heartwarming *Wonde...

item.title: FBI investigates Austin shooting after suspect spotted wearing Iranian flag and ‘Property of Allah’ sweatshirt
item.description: A shooting at Buford’s Backyard Beer Garden in Austin, Texas, left two dead and 14 others wounded on Sunday morning. The suspect, 53-year-old Ndiaga Diagne, was identified by authorities as the gunman...

item.title: Trump and Netanyahu’s Military Strike on Iran: A Breach of International Law
item.description: In his latest article, Kenneth Roth argues that the military action taken by Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu against Iran is an unlawful act of aggression, comparable to Russia’s invasion of Ukrai...

item.title: Trump Orders Military Strikes on Iran, Escalating Tensions in the Middle East
item.description: On the early morning of Saturday, February 28, 2026, President Donald Trump launched a massive military operation aimed at toppling the Iranian government. This assault, which includes bombings in Teh...

item.title: Human remains found in Sydney following mistaken identity kidnapping of 85-year-old man
item.description: Australian authorities have discovered human remains in connection with the kidnapping of 85-year-old widower Chris Baghsarian in North Ryde, Sydney. The elderly man was abducted just before dawn on 1...

item.title: US Orders Partial Evacuation of Beirut Embassy Amid Heightened Tensions with Iran
item.description: The US government has ordered a partial evacuation of its embassy in Beirut, Lebanon, citing a security review amid escalating tensions with Iran. Approximately 50 embassy staff have been instructed t...

item.title: Bellarmine Mugabe, youngest son of late Zimbabwean president, charged with attempted murder in Johannesburg shooting
item.description: Bellarmine Mugabe, the 28-year-old youngest son of Zimbabwe's late former President Robert Mugabe and his second wife Grace Mugabe, appeared briefly in a South African court in Johannesburg where he w...

item.title: US victims defrauded by Northern Ireland construction scam group
item.description: A New York woman, referred to as Jane, lost nearly $1 million to a scammer from Northern Ireland, who used the money to purchase a 10-carat diamond ring for his wife. The scammer, linked to a group ca...

item.title: How Incel Slang Has Spread to Mainstream Media and Government
item.description: In recent months, the use of 'incel' and 'looksmaxxing' slang has become more common, even in official government accounts. A tweet from the US Department of Defense boasting about military capabiliti...

item.title: UK retailers lock chocolate in anti-theft boxes due to rising theft
item.description: Retailers across the UK are increasingly securing chocolate bars in anti-theft boxes due to a surge in organised shoplifting. High-value chocolate, such as Cadbury Dairy Milk and Creme Eggs, has becom...

item.title: 16-year-old convicted for membership in banned neo-Nazi organisation
item.description: A 16-year-old boy from Northumberland has been found guilty of being a member of the banned extremist group The Base and of possessing and sharing terrorist publications. Leeds Crown Court delivered a...

item.title: How Structural Barriers Push Streaming Users Toward Piracy in MENA
item.description: In the Middle East and North Africa (MENA), access to streaming platforms remains uneven due to economic, political, and technological barriers. Countries like Syria and Lebanon face significant issue...

item.title: France Seeks to Restrict US Envoy Charles Kushner’s Access After Dispute Over Extremism Comments
item.description: A diplomatic dispute has emerged between France and the United States after comments posted by the US embassy in Paris sparked anger within the French government. The controversy began following the d...

item.title: Former Labour MP Jonathan Ashworth reveals stroke on New Year's Day caused by high blood pressure, urges blood pressure checks
item.description: Former Labour MP Jonathan Ashworth, who represented Leicester South from 2011 to 2024, has disclosed that he suffered an ischaemic stroke on New Year's Day 2026. The 47-year-old experienced stiffness ...

item.title: UK Parliament to Debate Release of Documents on Prince Andrew's Trade Envoy Role
item.description: The Liberal Democrats plan to push for the release of documents concerning Prince Andrew's appointment as a UK trade envoy, following his recent arrest. The party intends to table a motion in the Hous...

item.title: US-Israeli Strikes on Iran: A Breach of International Law and Diplomacy
item.description: The joint US-Israeli strikes on Iran in February 2025 are seen as a significant violation of international law. These attacks, launched while negotiations between the US and Iran were still active, ha...

item.title: England's Batting Woes Against Spin Threaten World Cup Progress
item.description: England's batting lineup continues to struggle against spin bowling in the ongoing T20 World Cup. Despite a 51-run victory over Sri Lanka, four of the top five England batters were dismissed by spinne...

item.title: UK Celebrates First Birth from Deceased Donor Womb Transplant
item.description: A baby boy named Hugo has become the first child in the UK born using a womb transplanted from a deceased donor. His mother, Grace Bell, in her 30s and born without a viable womb due to MRKH syndrome,...
